title: kstack script response envelope
description: Every kstack script (bin/entrypoint, skills/<name>/scripts/main) writes exactly one JSON object matching this schema to stdout and exits 0 on a clean run. Non-zero exits are reserved for unexpected crashes. The agent parses this envelope and dispatches based on `status` and `render`. Error categories let the agent present user-fixable problems differently from environment failures. Protocol version is carried in the `kstack` field.

kstack.description: Protocol version. Always the string "1" for v1.

status.description: Outcome. `ok` means the script ran to completion; `error` means it could not produce a response.

notice.description: Optional operator notice (update-available banner, etc.). When present the agent prepends it verbatim to its reply, above any `content` or `message`.

render.description: How the agent should handle `content`. `verbatim`: print `content` as-is and end the turn (fast path — no reformatting, no commentary). `agent`: treat `content` as tool output context and continue reasoning through the rest of SKILL.md; empty content is a preamble-only ack.

content.description: Payload. For `render:verbatim` this is exactly what should appear in the terminal. For `render:agent` this is tool output the agent reads (may be empty).

agent_context.description: Optional side-channel the agent reads but never shows the user. Use for structured metadata the agent needs for follow-ups (cache paths, context names, counts) that would clutter `content` if embedded there. Format is skill-specific — typically compact JSON the agent parses.

kind.description: `user`: user-fixable (bad flag, missing arg, invalid context). `infra`: environment/install failure (missing binary, broken cache, kubectl network error).

message.description: User-facing error message. The agent prints this verbatim and ends the turn.

examples.notice: kstack v2.0.0 is available (you're on v1.0.0). Say "upgrade kstack" to install, or "dismiss" to hide until the next release.

examples.message: Unable to list pods for context `prod`. kubectl exited non-zero.
